steps.title: Find the slope of line k from the graph
steps.content: Use Desmos to fit line $k$ from two clear graph points. The line passes through $(0,-5)$ and $(10,3)$. A linear regression gives slope $\frac{4}{5}$ and intercept $-5$.

steps.title: Apply the perpendicularity rule
steps.content: When two lines are perpendicular, their slopes are negative reciprocals. If line $k$ has slope $\frac{4}{5}$, then line $n$ has slope $-\frac{5}{4}$. This eliminates choices C and D, which have positive slopes.

steps.title: Use the point (4, 7) to find the y-intercept
steps.content: Line $n$ passes through $(4,7)$ with slope $-\frac{5}{4}$. Using point-slope form: $$y-7=-\frac{5}{4}(x-4)$$ $$y=-\frac{5}{4}x+12$$ That matches choice B, so B is correct.
